chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجُوزُ مِنَ الْوَقْفِ وَ الصَّدَقَةِ وَ النُّحْلِ وَ الْهِبَةِ وَ السُّكْنَى وَ الْعُمْرَى وَ الرُّقْبَى وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ عَلَى الْوَلَدِ وَ غَيْرِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 23 – What is allowed from the endowment, and the charity, and the grant, and the gift, and the (provision) of habitation, and the lifetime (service) and the bondman, and what is allowed from that upon the child and others

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/52)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/52)
text: إِنْ تَصَدَّقْتَ بِصَدَقَةٍ لَمْ تَرْجِعْ إِلَيْكَ وَ لَمْ تَشْتَرِهَا إِلَّا أَنْ تُورَثَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Abdullah Bin Al Mugheira, from Mansour Bin Hazim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When you are given in charity with a charity, it does not return back to you, and you do not buy it except if you were to inherit it’.
en.sarwar: 8. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ‘ Abd Allah ibn al-Mughirah from Mansur ibn Hazim who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If you give charity, then do not take it back or buy it back unless it comes back to you in the form of an inherited legacy.”’
